seoTitle: Add Ranking, Likert, and Rating Fields in Forms
metaDescription: Combine Ranking, Likert, and Rating question types in one Microsoft Forms survey to capture priorities, sentiment, and satisfaction scores.
intro: Different kinds of feedback call for different question formats — a star rating for quick satisfaction, a Likert grid for multi-statement sentiment, and drag-and-drop ranking for prioritization. This guide shows how to add all three types in a single Microsoft Forms survey.

steps.heading: Add a Rating question
steps.body: Click Add New and choose Rating, then use the Levels and Symbol dropdowns to set your scale count and icon style, such as stars, numbers, or hearts.

steps.heading: Open the extended question types
steps.body: Click Add New again and use the More Question Types down arrow to open the extended menu.

steps.heading: Insert a Likert matrix
steps.body: Select Likert from the extended list, then edit the horizontal scale columns and vertical statement rows to fit your evaluation criteria.

steps.heading: Add a Ranking question
steps.body: Open the extended drawer again and select Ranking to insert a drag-and-drop item list that lets respondents reorder their preferences.

steps.heading: Require and preview all fields
steps.body: Toggle Required across your new questions, then use Preview to confirm each element renders and responds correctly.

faq.question: What's the difference between Rating and Likert?
faq.answer: Rating captures a single score using stars or symbols, while Likert evaluates multiple related statements in one grid.

faq.question: How does the Ranking question work for respondents?
faq.answer: Respondents drag and drop items into their preferred order, which the survey then records as a ranked list.

faq.question: Can I change the rating symbol from stars to something else?
faq.answer: Yes, the Symbol dropdown lets you choose between stars, numbers, hearts, or thumbs.

faq.question: Are these advanced question types available in quizzes too?
faq.answer: Yes, Rating, Likert, and Ranking are all available inside both standard forms and quizzes.
